body:
## Summary

- Adds `tools/benchmark/perf-suite/` — a **compile-time** performance harness (it measures how long `slangc` takes to compile, not GPU/runtime), to **detect and localize compiler-time regressions** and compare a build against historical releases.
- **26 GPU-free, headless workloads** — per-stage stressors, type-checking/overload probes, shared-infrastructure/floor canaries, coverage-gap probes, source-emit (Metal/WGSL), a scaling ladder, and the real **`mdl_dxr`** shader corpus.
- **Local dev tooling**: `bench.py` (run the suite), `compare_branches.py` / `compare.py` (branch-vs-branch — did a change cost compile time), `sweep_report.py` (scaling), and `analyze.py` + `report.py` + `breakdown.py` .
- **Two CI workflows** (on the dedicated nvrgfx-perf runner pool) that build the nightly compile-time series in a results repo — present but **not yet auto-enabled**.

## Motivation

Slang has a runtime/MDL benchmark (`tools/benchmark/compile.py` + `benchmark.yml`) but **no compiler-time perf harness** — no way to catch a compile-time regression, attribute it to a specific compiler stage, or pin it to the release that introduced it. Compile time matters for large downstream shader codebases; this suite fills that gap and makes such regressions reproducible and attributable.

## Technical Details

**Measuremets.** Runs `slangc -report-perf-benchmark` and captures _all_ per-phase timers (`[*] <phase> <count> <ms>`).

- Headline metric `compileInner` (front-end + IR + codegen), which excludes the fixed core-module-load floor, so it is stable and comparable across releases.
- Localization via the **nested** Slang-internal stage timers attributed to **leaf** nodes (`generateOutput → linkAndOptimizeIR → specializeModule`, …), measured before any downstream tool.
- `1 warmup + N timed` runs (default 5); the **median** is the saved/compared value (steadier than min when run-to-run spread shifts). `results.json` keeps median/min/mean/stdev per timer; the reporting tools take `--metric`.
- Peak RSS is captured when GNU `/usr/bin/time` is present (POSIX only — not collected on Windows, and not yet surfaced in any report).

**Workloads** (26; deterministic + size-scaled in `workloads.py`, declared in `manifest.py`): stage stressors (autodiff, dynamic_dispatch, specialization, inlining, parse, sema_generics, codegen_spirv, module_link); type-checking probes (operator_typecheck, implicit_conversion, overload_resolution); shared-infra/floor canaries (minimal, ir_builder, serialize, conformance, loop_unroll); coverage-gap probes (existential_aggregate, resource_aggregate, reflection_layout, control_flow_ssa, and emit_metal/emit_wgsl for the source-emit backend `-emit-spirv-directly` skips); a complexity_ladder; the diagnostics_errors/clean size-matched control pair; and the real **`mdl_dxr`** shader. All GPU-free / external-SDK-free.

**Reporting.** `analyze.py` → per-`(workload,timer)` series + STEP/DRIFT/FASTER classification + leaf attribution; `plot.py` → combined (normalized + log) and per-benchmark linear charts; `report.py` → a self-contained cross-release HTML report (+ a per-benchmark page); `breakdown.py` → per-phase attribution; `sweep_report.py` → `floor + k·N` scaling for a single build. Design rationale, data model, and result layout are in **`DESIGN.md`**.

**CI workflows.** Both run on the dedicated, quiesced **NVIDIA RGFX perf pool** (runner group `nvrgfx`, labels `[Windows, X64, nvrgfx-perf]` — the same machines slangpy's benchmark uses) and push results to a dedicated repo **`shader-slang/slang-compile-perf`** (authenticated with the `SLANG_COMPILE_PERF_PAT` secret), mirroring the `push-benchmark-results.yml` → `slang-material-modules-benchmark` pattern:

- **Nightly ToT** (`.github/workflows/compile-perf-nightly.yml`): builds master HEAD, sweeps into `daily/<date>-<sha>/`, rebuilds the tracking series (`track.py`), pushes, then `trend.py` fails the job (with `::error::` annotations) when a primary timer drifts past the trailing-median threshold. **`workflow_dispatch`-only for now — the daily `schedule` is commented out**, to be enabled once the suite is validated on the runner and the history is seeded.
- **Release sweep** (`.github/workflows/compile-perf-release-sweep.yml`, `workflow_dispatch`): builds the per-release history baseline on the runner (run with `force=true` to resync onto a new machine).

**Per-PR gate — designed, deferred.** A fast soft-fail per-PR gate (build the PR head + its merge-base on the same runner, diff with `compare.py`, non-blocking) is documented in `DESIGN.md` but **intentionally not part of this PR**. `compare.py` / `compare_branches.py` already provide that check locally today.

## Test Plan

Tooling only — no compiler code and no existing tests change, so there are no new `.slang` regression tests.

- [x] All `perf-suite/*.py` `python3 -m py_compile` clean; `-h`/`--help` prints usage for every runnable script.
- [x] Docs + the workflow YAMLs pass the repo's prettier check; the referenced `./.github/actions/common-setup` composite action exists on master.
- [x] Cross-platform: `fetch_releases.py` is platform-aware (Windows `.zip` / Linux `.tar.gz`, picks `slangc.exe`); `bench.py` degrades cleanly where GNU `/usr/bin/time` is absent (Windows — no RSS, timing unaffected).
- [x] Suite + HTML reports generated against a dev build and a multi-release sweep; STEP/DRIFT/FASTER classification, leaf-attributed flags, and scaling fits produced as documented.
- [ ] Nightly / release-sweep exercised end-to-end on the runner — pending nvrgfx runner-group access.

Related to #11501
title: Add compile-time performance test suite
